zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de Say III於1209年2月12日出生在英國英格蘭肯特韋斯特 格林維其時，他的父親Geoffrey II de Saye54歲，母親Hawise de Clare21歲。他於大約1230年與Sibyl Marshal結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1272年2月12日在英國賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯享年63歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William de Say III于1209年2月12日出生在英国英格兰肯特韋斯特 格林維其时，他的父亲Geoffrey II de Saye54岁，母亲Hawise de Clare21岁。他于大约1230年与Sibyl Marshal结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1272年2月12日在英国賀特福爾薩布利其沃斯享年63岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Say
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sey
meanings: English and Scottish: probably a variant of   Say   particularly prevalent in Scotland.
Dutch and Flemish: from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name Sigiheri (see Seger ).
West African (Ghana): Akan (Fante) name of unexplained etymology.

variants.name: Sayer
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Saye
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Say III was born on 12 February 1209, in West Greenwich, Kent, England, United Kingdom, his father, Geoffrey II de Saye, was 54 and his mother, Hawise de Clare, was 21. He married Sibyl Marshal about 1230. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died about 12 February 1272, in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de Say III est né le 12 février 1209, à West Greenwich, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Geoffrey II de Saye, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Hawise de Clare, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Sibyl Marshal environ 1230. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé environ 12 février 1272, à Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans.

Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb um 12. Februar 1272 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Sawbridgeworth, Hertfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William de Say IIIが1209年2月12日にイギリスイギリスケントウエスト グレエンウィチで生まれた時，父Geoffrey II de Sayeは54歳，母Hawise de Clareは21歳であった。約1230年にSibyl Marshalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は約1272年2月12日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアサウブリジェウオルスで63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Say III(이)가 1209년 2월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 웨스트 그린위치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Geoffrey II de Saye(은)는 54세였고, 그의 어머니 Hawise de Clare(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1230년 Sibyl Marshal(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1272년 2월 12일 영국 허트퍼드실르 사w브리지워스에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de Say III родился 12 февраля 1209 г. в местности вест гринвич, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Geoffrey II de Saye было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Hawise de Clare, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Sibyl Marshal около 1230 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер около 12 февраля 1272 г. в местности савбриджюорт, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 63 лет.
